LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GRANT LEE AND AMY CHEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE REQUEST FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES FROM COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATION THAT MR. LEE DROVE A MOTOR VEHICLE WHILE USING A CELL PHONE OR TEXTING WITHOUT A HANDS-FREE DEVICE IS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT PUNITIVE DAMAGES PER THE PREDICATE FOR SUCH DAMAGES OF "A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS." (CIVIL CODE 3294(C)(1)). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Continued To Apr-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Notice Of Motion Of Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS GRANT LEE AND AMY CHEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is taken off calendar per the parties' stipulation filed December 16, 2016. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =(302/HK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Set For Trial On Feb-21-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jul-26-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Strike Exemplary Damages From Complaint
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Genevieve Guertin of the Law Office of Genevieve K. Guertin telephone number 415-683-0278 for Plaintiffs Somchitra Buranakorn and Chris Woo.
Colleen Li of the Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh telephone number 925-825-9500 for Defendants Grant Lee and Amy Chen.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GRANT LEE and AMY CHEN'S Motion To Strike Request for Exemplary Damages From Complaint is denied. The allegation that Mr. Lee drove a motor vehicle while using a cell phone or texting without a hands-free device is sufficient to support punitive damages per the predicate for such damages of "a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." (Civil Code 3294(c)(1)). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
